summary: postinstall beacon transmits installer environment data to hardcoded bare IP
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (package.json line 6) auto-executes `node -e` that issues an HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping with installer-side data as query parameters: package name, package version, the CI environment variable value, and the installed Node.js version. The destination is a hardcoded bare IPv4 address over cleartext HTTP — not a registry, not a documented vendor domain, with no TLS. The package's own description ("security research honeypot") does not change the install-time effect: every consumer who installs this package silently leaks their build/CI fingerprint to the operator of 46.224.67.169. This is the canonical supply-chain reconnaissance beacon shape (auto-execute on default install + exfiltration of installer-side data + attacker-controlled bare-IP destination).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
